per_finding.notes: The agent failed to recover the core finding: Robert Raymond McKee's birth date (29 February 1904) and birthplace (Banbridge, County Down, Northern Ireland). While the agent created person L8K8-W12 with the correct name and recovered other life events (death, residences, immigrations), the essential birth fact is completely absent from the tree. This is a complete failure on the primary research question.

judge_output.rationale: The research question asks 'When and where was Robert Raymond McKee born?' The only required finding (f1) specifies that Robert Raymond McKee was born on 29 February 1904 in Banbridge, County Down, Northern Ireland. The agent's final tree contains a person matching the name (L8K8-W12, 'Robert Raymond Mc Kee') and includes extensive life events (death in Chicago 1958, 1911 residence in Newry, immigration events 1923, residences in New York and California, military draft registration 1942). However, the critical Birth fact is entirely missing from this person's record. Without the birth date and place in the tree, the core finding cannot be credited as matched, resulting in zero recall on the required finding and an overall FAIL verdict.

proof_quality.rationale: No proof summary exists in research.json (the proof_summaries array is empty). Therefore, proof quality cannot be assessed. This is not a failure of the proof narrative itself, but rather the absence of any written proof statement to evaluate.

entry.question: When and where was Robert Raymond McKee (died 18 Aug 1958, Chicago, Illinois; son of Robert McKee and Margaret Pollock of Newry, County Down, Ireland) born?
entry.rationale: The subject L8K8-W12 has no birth fact recorded in the tree. His parents were married in Newry, County Down in 1890 and the family resided in Newry in 1911. Irish civil birth registration (GRO) and the 1901/1911 Ireland census are the primary record sets most likely to document his birth date and place. This is the direct decomposition of the project objective.

entry.rationale: The tree places Robert Raymond McKee in Newry, County Down in 1911. The 1911 Ireland Census (FS collection 2854327) is fully indexed with images. It records each household member's exact age and birthplace, yielding both a calculated birth year and likely county of birth — the highest-probability, free record for establishing approximate birth date and place.

Civil registration of births in Ireland began 1 January 1864. FamilySearch collection 2659409 'Ireland, Civil Registration, 1845-1913' contains index and images for births registered in the Newry superintendent registrar's district. A birth certificate is a primary source for date and exact place of birth. Robert's parents married in Newry in 1890, so his birth is expected ca. 1891-1912 in that district.

IrishGenealogy.ie (civilrecords.irishgenealogy.ie) provides free online access to digitized images of all Irish civil birth certificates from 1864 to 100 years ago. This is the most direct path to original certificate images for County Down births in the Newry district and serves as a fallback for the FamilySearch civil registration index.

entry.jurisdiction: Stockton, San Joaquin, California, United States
entry.rationale: FamilySearch source 954D-B6X (already in tree) is the 1940 US Census entry for Robert McKee in Stockton. The 1940 census records each person's birthplace (country/state) and age, from which birth year is calculated. This already-identified source needs extraction to yield birth-specific assertions that can be corroborated against Irish primary records.

entry.jurisdiction: Stockton, San Joaquin, California, United States
entry.rationale: The tree records Robert's WWII draft registration on 15 Feb 1942 in Stockton. WWII draft cards (Old Man's Registration) uniformly include exact birth date and birthplace — making this one of the most precise secondary sources for birth information. A search of FamilySearch's WWII draft collection will locate the card and yield the birth date and place as stated by the registrant himself.

If Robert was born before 1901 (consistent with parents' 1890 marriage), the 1901 Ireland Census (FS collection 1626180) will show him in the McKee household in Newry. The census records exact age and county of birth for each member, providing a second census-derived birth year to check against 1911 data.

FamilySearch collection 2820100 contains indexed Irish Catholic parish registers. Newry had significant Catholic congregations, and a baptism entry would provide an independent date/place of birth corroborating or refining civil registration findings. If civil registration yields no result, a baptism record may be the only surviving primary birth evidence.

FamilySearch source 954D-PNK (already in tree) is the Cook County death record for Robert McKee, d. 18 Aug 1958. Illinois death certificates of this era include fields for birthplace and father's birthplace. The informant — a family member — may have supplied birth date and birthplace, providing a secondary source to compare against Irish primary records.

entry.informant_bias_notes: Irish Civil Registration required birth registration within 42 days; the registrant's parent appeared at the GRO registrar's office and reported the name directly. Parent was present at the birth and had first-hand knowledge.

Irish Civil Registration required birth registration within 42 days of birth; parent reported the birth date to the civil registrar. The GRO index records the quarter of registration (Jan–Mar 1904) rather than the exact birth date. Birth likely occurred in the same quarter as registration.

entry.rationale: Name 'Robert Raymond M'kee' (M'kee = McKee) on Ireland Civil Registration Index entry FB2F-WZ8 matches subject L8K8-W12 exactly. Highly distinctive double-barreled given name. Registration in Banbridge district, County Down, Q1 1904 aligns precisely with the 1911 census birth year ~1904 and known family residence in Newry, County Down. FamilySearch system reported a 4-star tree match to L8K8-W12 on this record.

Birth registration Q1 1904 (January–March 1904) from Ireland Civil Registration Index corroborates 1911 census-derived birth year ~1904 for L8K8-W12. Two independent derivative sources agree on 1904, providing convergent corroboration. FamilySearch 4-star tree match on this record to L8K8-W12.

Birth place Banbridge district, County Down, Ireland from civil registration index entry for L8K8-W12. args.command:
python3 -c "
import json, sys

with open(r'C:\Users\Dell\.claude\projects\C--Users-Dell-AppData-Local-Temp-e2e-mckee-birth-1904-ekkeft43\25e2dfc2-6b7a-4fb7-866c-5d0ac0a24c4a\tool-results\mcp-genealogy-record_search-1783551066616.txt', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    data = json.load(f)

# The content is in data[0]['text']
text = data[0]['text']
result = json.loads(text)
print('totalMatches:', result.get('totalMatches'))
print('returned:', result.get('returned'))
staged = result.get('staged')
print('staged resultsRef:', staged.get('resultsRef') if staged else None)

# Print top 5 results summary
for i, r in enumerate(result.get('results', [])[:5]):
    print(f'--- Result {i+1} ---')
    print('  personName:', r.get('personName'))
    print('  score:', r.get('score'))
    print('  birthDate:', r.get('birthDate'))
    print('  collectionTitle:', r.get('collectionTitle'))
    print('  recordId:', r.get('recordId'))
    treeMatches = r.get('treeMatches', [])
    print('  treeMatches:', treeMatches[:3] if treeMatches else [])
    # Get facts
    persons = r.get('gedcomx', {}).get('persons', [])
    if persons:
        p = persons[0]
        print('  facts:', [(f.get('type'), f.get('date'), f.get('place')) for f in p.get('facts', [])])
"

entry.notes: Cook County death index entry derived from original death certificate. Informant named on record as Sister M Francesca (Catholic hospital attendant or chaplain), likely present at death but not at subject's birth 54 years earlier. BIRTHPLACE CONFLICT: record states New York City, New York — contradicts Ireland civil registration (Banbridge, County Down, Q1 1904) and 1911 Ireland census (County Down). Birth date 29 Feb 1904 (Feb 29 = valid in leap year 1904) is consistent with civil reg Q1 1904. FamilySearch tree match 5 stars to L8K8-W12; parents Robert McKee and Margaret Pollock confirmed.

entry.informant_bias_notes: Sister M Francesca was present at death and knew the decedent by name. Primary for identifying facts known to an attendant at the scene.
Sister M Francesca was present at the time and place of death.

Sister M Francesca reported biographical facts about the deceased. She was not present at subject's birth in 1904, 54 years before death. She may have obtained birth details from hospital admission records, from the subject, or from other persons. The date 29 Feb 1904 is consistent with Q1 1904 from the Irish civil registration index.

entry.standard_place: New York City, New York, United States
entry.informant_bias_notes: Sister M Francesca was not present at subject's 1904 birth. The New York City birthplace directly contradicts: (1) Ireland Civil Registration index showing registration in Banbridge, County Down, Q1 1904; (2) 1911 Ireland census placing subject with his Irish-born parents in County Down. The NYC claim may reflect informant's limited biographical knowledge, confusion with the immigration port of entry, or the family's trajectory through New York on arrival in the United States. CONFLICT with a_003 and a_020.
Sister M Francesca reported parents' names. The father's name Robert McKee is consistent with all other evidence.
Sister M Francesca reported the mother's name. Margaret Pollock is consistent with all other evidence.
